title: Chapter 7: The Wall-Slam
content: Day 10. The apocalypse was ten days old, and Marcus Chen was about to do the single most terrifying thing in his life.
Not fighting zombies. Not venturing into the parking garage. Not even facing Derek Cole.
This was worse.

**[QUEST: Heartbeat Race]**
**[Objective: Perform a wall-slam on Seraphina Vale and maintain deep eye contact for 10 seconds.]**
**[Condition: Must be initiated during a moment of genuine tension.]**
**[Risk Assessment: If Affection is insufficient, she may reflexively attack — 45% chance of lethal response]**
**[Reward: Infinite Power Generator (Solar-Fusion, no fuel required)]**
**[Bonus: If completed without triggering combat reflex, Affection +10]**
Wall-slam. A *wall-slam*.

Marcus sat on the edge of his bed, reading the System text for the fourth time, trying to convince himself it said something else. "Deliver a friendly handshake" maybe. Or "Wave politely from a safe distance."
"Wall-slam the zombie queen." He pressed his palms to his eyes. "The zombie queen who can move faster than sight. The zombie queen who just got tactical armor. The zombie queen who—"
"Who is standing right here and can hear you," Sera said from the doorway.

She was dressed in the Shadow Weave, and somehow the outfit made her even more striking—a shadow with cheekbones, lethal and lovely, leaning against the doorframe with an ease that suggested she hadn't spent the last decade as a mindless corpse.
"System quest," he said, gesturing at the floating text. "Wall-slam. Ten seconds of eye contact. Forty-five percent chance you bite my throat."

Sera studied the quest description—for her, the System's golden glow translated into emotional impressions, like reading Braille through feeling rather than touch. Her expression shifted from curiosity to something harder to name.
"A test," she said. "The System wants to see if I've evolved past my reflexes. If I can override the instinct to attack when a larger creature suddenly invades my space."
"Or it wants me dead. Fifty-fifty."

"My Affection for you isn't at fifty. My *trust* in you—" She stepped closer. "—is absolute. I've killed for you. I've *spoken* for you. My instincts know your scent, your heartbeat, the particular rhythm of your breathing when you're scared. They know you're not a threat."
"So you're saying I should just do it?"
"I'm saying that if you hesitate, it'll read as uncertainty, and uncertainty *will* trigger my reflexes. If you commit—" Her grey eyes locked onto his. "—I'll catch you. Every time."

**[Affection: 45/100]**
**[Seraphina's confidence in your bond is high. Combat reflex probability is lower than 45% — but the System always rounds up for danger.]**
Great. Rounding up. Just like his student loans.
He stood. Sera didn't move—didn't step back, didn't shift into a defensive posture. She stood in the middle of the apartment like she was waiting for something, grey eyes steady, composed, trusting.

Trusting *him*. Marcus Chen. The guy who'd never kissed anyone. The guy who'd once hidden in a bathroom during a company icebreaker. The guy whose cardiovascular system was currently auditioning for a drum solo.
"Okay," he said. "Okay. Wall-slam. I can do a wall-slam. I've seen them in anime. It's just—"
He moved.

Not a walk. Not a hesitant shuffle. Something between instinct and desperation carried him across the room in two steps, and his hand was on the wall beside her head before his brain caught up to his body, and she was against the plaster and he was *close*—close enough to see the individual grey strands in her irises, close enough to smell the cold-flower-copper scent of her, close enough to feel the absolute stillness of a body that didn't breathe unless it chose to.
Her pupils blew wide.

For one frozen instant, Marcus saw the predator behind the woman—the ancient, hungry thing that lived in the deeper layers of her brain, the thing that had spent eight days as a mindless eating machine before system food woke it up. Her lips parted. Her jaw shifted a fraction of an inch.
The reflex. The bite.
He didn't pull away.

He looked into her eyes—really looked, past the grey and the hunger and the centuries of instinct—and saw *her*. Sera. The woman who'd said his name like it mattered. The woman who'd killed for him without being asked. The woman who was fighting every molecule of her undead biology to stay *still* beneath his hand instead of giving in to what her body wanted to do.
Her jaw clenched. Her fingers dug into her own thighs so hard the Shadow Weave creaked.
"One," Marcus whispered. "Two. Three—"
Her breathing—not that she needed to breathe—came in sharp, controlled bursts. Her eyes never left his. The grey was being swallowed by pupil, the predator surging, *wanting*—
"Four. Five. Six—"
A sound escaped her throat. Not a growl. A whimper. The sound of something ancient and hungry being denied its nature by something stronger: choice.
"Seven. Eight—"

Her hand came up—fast, faster than he could track—and he flinched, muscle memory screaming *she's going to claw your face off*—but her fingers didn't go for his throat. They went for his collar. She grabbed a fistful of his shirt and pulled him *closer*, closing the distance between them until his forehead was against hers, until her cold breath ghosted across his lips, until the only thing in his world was grey eyes and the scent of cold flowers and the trembling of a zombie queen who was choosing not to bite.
"Nine," she breathed. The word was a razor's edge. "Ten."

**[MILESTONE COMPLETE: Heartbeat Race]**
**[Wall-slam duration: 12.3 seconds (exceeded requirement)]**
**[Combat Reflex Triggered: NO]**
**[Assessment: Seraphina Vale has FULLY overridden her predatory instincts through emotional bond strength.]**
**[This is unprecedented. Stage 2 zombies do not typically override combat reflexes.]**
**[Your bond is abnormal. The System approves.]**

**[Reward Delivered: Infinite Power Generator (Solar-Fusion)]**
**[- Installed on apartment rooftop (automatic)]**
**[- Output: 50kW continuous, no fuel, no maintenance, zero emissions]**
**[- Powers apartment complex indefinitely]**

**[Bonus: Affection +10]**
**[Affection: 45 → 55/100]**
**[THRESHOLD REACHED: Stage 3 Evolution Available]**
Marcus's forehead was still pressed against hers. His heart was trying to exit his body through his sternum. Her fingers were still knotted in his shirt, and she hadn't let go.
"That was—" he started.
"Terrifying," she finished. "For both of us."
"You were scared?"

"I was terrified I'd hurt you." Her voice was barely a whisper. "My body wanted to bite. It *needed* to bite. And I told it no. Because you're more important than instinct." A pause. Her grip tightened. "Don't ever make me do that again."
"I won't."

"Good." She released his collar. Stepped back. Composed herself with visible effort—you could almost see the CEO reassembling, posture straightening, expression smoothing. "Now. We have power. We have a generator. We have a fortress in the making."
"And you have an evolution available."

Sera touched her own chest, over where a heartbeat should be. "I can feel it. The next stage. It's like a door inside me, and it's *almost* open." Her eyes found his. "Feed me the Prime Spirit Meat, and I'll walk through it."
"You'll be okay?"
"I was a CEO, Marcus. I've walked through worse doors." The ghost of a smile. "Just don't let go this time."
He wouldn't. He knew that now.
Marcus held out the Prime Spirit Meat, and Sera took both the meat and his hand, and her eyes closed, and the evolution began.

**[SERAPHINA VALE — Stage 3 Evolution Initiating]**
**[Estimated Duration: 6 hours]**
**[New abilities on completion: Full Memory Recall, Supernatural Speed, Zombie Queen Authority]**
The lights flickered on—the apartment's fluorescents blazing to life for the first time in seven days, powered by a generator that would never run out.
In the sudden glare, Sera clutched his hand, trembling, evolving, and Marcus didn't let go.

He had six hours until she woke up. Six hours to plan the rescue. Six hours until he walked into that parking garage with a zombie queen at his side and proved to Elena Reyes that some doors were worth walking through.

**[NEW QUEST UNLOCKED]**
**[QUEST: Dinner and a Show]**
**[Prerequisite: Complete before confronting Derek]**
**[Objective: Prepare a meal using System ingredients and bring it to the parking garage.]**
**[Purpose: Demonstrate resource superiority and force Derek to acknowledge your power.]**
**[Reward: 1x System SUV (Armored), 1x Gourmet Meal Kit (6 servings)]**
**[This quest will take you to the confrontation. Choose your moment carefully.]**
The SUV would be the entrance. The meal would be the statement.
And Derek Cole was about to learn that the apocalypse had a new apex predator—and it wasn't him.
